title: Hibiscus ricinifolius
taxonomy: Hibiscus ricinifolius is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It belongs to the genus Hibiscus and is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Australia. This species is known for its large, showy flowers and is often used as an ornamental plant. Hibiscus ricinifolius is also sometimes referred to as the 'hairy hibiscus' due to its distinctive hairy leaves. The plant is also known to have medicinal properties and is used in traditional medicine in some parts of the world.
uses: Hibiscus ricinifolius has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. intro: Hibiscus ricinifolius, also known as the 'Tear-thorn Hibiscus', is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family, characterized by its distinctive, ovate-shaped leaves and showy, funnel-shaped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, alleviating fever, and acting as a natural diuretic to help manage hypertension and urinary disorders. The bioactive constituents of Hibiscus ricinifolius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies like oils and ointments, to promote overall well-being and treat a range of health issues.
